verdicts.reason: Quote verified at FAB Sons-p01:149; MOTIVATES type correct (Gyldayn explicitly frames bequest as feeding Maegor's motivation, but action is years later and Maegor's free choice).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:81; TRIGGERS type correct (Visenya's fetching within one hour = immediate next beat, no decisional gap).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:89; CAUSES type correct (Maegor's decree is direct real-causation response to Gawen's objection).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:175; MOTIVATES type correct (Aegon's rebellion is free political decision in response to usurpation).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:49; MOTIVATES type correct (public humiliation feeds grievance, but Maegor's later claim is his own choice).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:49; TRIGGERS type correct ('at the feast afterward' = very next beat).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:61; CAUSES type correct (multiple intermediate steps: Murmison performing rite, expulsion, High Septon's denunciation, then Poor Fellows act).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:61; CAUSES type correct (same radicalization wave escalates to attack on king; Kingsguard intervention doesn't negate causation).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p01:237; MOTIVATES type correct (ultimatum drives Maegor's choice to exile, but choice is his own).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:135; CAUSES type correct (28 days recovery between trial and burning; multi-step causal chain, not instant trigger).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:143; MOTIVATES type correct (High Septon's decision to call arms is third party's free choice, not automatic consequence).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p02:201; ENABLES type correct (victory is precondition for burnings but doesn't force timing/occurrence; Visenya's decision completes causation).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:23; ENABLES type correct (Maegor's absence is opportunity for Aegon's seizing; Aegon's decision completes causation).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:25; PART_OF type correct (dragon theft is opening beat of wider rebellion).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:175; ENABLES type correct (battle's dead create pool of available brides, but Maegor's decision to marry is his own).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:93; CAUSES type correct (stillbirth sets off textually explicit chain: suspicion → torture → confessions → arrest/execution).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:101; CAUSES type correct (Alys's death is framed as direct continuation of same purge).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:87; ENABLES type correct (Lucas's position as Hand gives access/opportunity for scheme, but scheme is Lucas & Alys's decision).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:87; MOTIVATES type correct (Ceryse's return drives Alys's fear-based decision to seek heir by illicit means).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:193; TRIGGERS type correct ('in the aftermath of the wedding' = very next beat of same occasion).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:195; MOTIVATES type correct (disinheritance is grievance, but uprising is deliberate political act by Rogar/Jaehaerys).

Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:209; tier-2 + disputed:true + in_universe_source:gyldayn-synthesis correct. Hedge is real ('even in death,' 'revenge,' 'came to pass' without stated mechanism). CAUSES type correct (no living decision mediates; Tyanna already dead). Supernatural/folkloric framing explicitly uncertain per Gyldayn.
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:213; MOTIVATES type correct (perception that king is 'accursed' is psychological opening for lords' defection, a political decision).
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:219; TRIGGERS type correct (Rhaena's flight is immediate next beat upon hearing news, no other decision intervenes).

Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:241; tier-2 + disputed:true + in_universe_source:gyldayn-synthesis correct. Hedge is real ('Many to this day believe,' text offers multiple conflicting theories of death mechanism: Iron Throne, Elinor, Kingsguard, suicide, unknown). CAUSES type correct for uprising→death (causal frame is collapse of support), but literal death mechanism explicitly disputed in text per Gyldayn.
Quote verified at FAB Sons-p03:121; ENABLES type correct (ashes/ruins are physical precondition for Dragonpit construction, but Maegor's decision to build there is his own).
Quote verified at FAB Prince-p06:41; MOTIVATES type correct (Rosby's suicide is his free decision made in response to king's death, preserving agency per rubric).
Quote verified at FAB Prince-p06:149; ENABLES type correct (Moon's murder 'removed obstacle' per Gyldayn's own word; coronation still requires travel & rite by multiple parties).
Quote verified at FAB Prince-p06:137; TRIGGERS type correct (Lorcas's attack follows directly from power vacuum & corpse-dispute created by Moon's murder, next beat).

Quote verified at FAB Prince-p06:185; tier-2 + disputed:true + in_universe_source:gyldayn-synthesis correct. Hedge is real ('Though nothing was ever proved,' 'it was widely believed'). ENABLES type correct (coronation creates opportunity with both twins present, one returning to court; switch is Rhaena/Alyssa's alleged decision, not compelled by coronation). Text says 'mayhaps' and frames as speculation despite possibility of identity swap changing history.

summary: B0506 VERIFICATION COMPLETE. 30/30 edges CONFIRM. 4/4 new_nodes OK. All three tier-2/disputed edges verified: B-E22 (Tyanna's posthumous revenge) correctly marked as uncertain poisoning mechanism vs. coincidence; B-E25 (Maegor's death) correctly marked with four competing theories per text; B-E30 (twin switch) correctly marked as unproven but widely believed speculation. Hedge language present in all disputed rows. Quote anchoring verified in source chapters. Type assignments all appropriate per VERIFY-RULES rubric (MOTIVATES vs CAUSES/TRIGGERS, ENABLES for preconditions with external agency, role/part-of structural edges correct).
